metadata.title: Kaura Local Government
metadata.description: Official website of Kaura Local Government Area, Kaduna State, Nigeria.

slides.title: Kaura – Cultural Heritage of Southern Kaduna
slides.subtitle: Kaura Local Government Area is known for its rich cultural traditions, fertile agricultural land, and vibrant communities.

slides.title: Kaura – A Land of Agriculture and Tradition
slides.subtitle: Farmers across Kaura cultivate crops that contribute significantly to the agricultural output of Kaduna State.

paragraphs: Kaura Local Government Area is located in the southern part of Kaduna State, Nigeria. The administrative headquarters is Kaura town.
The LGA is home to several ethnic groups including the Atyap, Bajju, Agworok (Kagoro), Hausa, and Fulani, creating a culturally diverse region.
Agriculture is the major economic activity, with crops such as maize, ginger, millet, sorghum, and groundnuts cultivated across the area.

chairman.name: Executive Chairman, Kaura Local Government
messageParagraphs: Welcome to the official website of Kaura Local Government Area. Our administration is committed to improving infrastructure, education, healthcare, and economic opportunities for residents.
Kaura is blessed with fertile land and hardworking communities who contribute significantly to agricultural production within Kaduna State.
Through transparent governance and inclusive development, we aim to build a prosperous future for all citizens of Kaura.

cards.title: Maize Farming
cards.subtitle: Staple Crop
cards.description: Maize is widely cultivated across Kaura communities and remains a staple food crop.

cards.title: Ginger Farming
cards.subtitle: Major Cash Crop
cards.description: Farmers in Kaura cultivate ginger as a major cash crop contributing to Kaduna State's agricultural economy.

cards.title: Livestock Rearing
cards.subtitle: Animal Husbandry
cards.description: Livestock such as cattle, goats, sheep, and poultry are raised across rural communities within Kaura LGA.

news.subtitle: Stay informed about development projects, community programs, and government initiatives in Kaura.

hero.title: About Kaura
hero.subtitle: Discover the culture, geography, and economy of Kaura Local Government Area.

location: Neighboring LGAs: Jema'a, Zangon Kataf, and Sanga.
demographics: Major Ethnic Groups: Atyap, Bajju, Agworok (Kagoro), Hausa, Fulani.
Languages Spoken: Atyap, Bajju, Hausa, and English.
economy: Main Economic Activities: Farming, livestock rearing, and trading.
Climate: Tropical savannah climate suitable for agriculture.

geographyEconomy.title: Geography & Economy
geographyEconomy.location: Kaura lies within the southern Kaduna region and has fertile land suitable for grain and cash crop production.
geographyEconomy.climate: The area experiences a rainy season between May and October and a dry season between November and April.
mainActivities: Crop Farming: Maize, millet, sorghum, ginger, and groundnuts.
Livestock Rearing: Cattle, goats, sheep, and poultry.
Local Markets: Agricultural produce trading across communities.

hero.title: Government
hero.subtitle: Leadership and administrative structure of Kaura Local Government.

structure.intro: Kaura Local Government operates through an executive leadership supported by a legislative council and administrative departments.
executive: Supervisory Councilors responsible for major sectors.
legislative: The Legislative Council consists of elected councilors representing wards in Kaura.
A Speaker elected among the councilors presides over legislative sessions.

hero.title: Development Projects
hero.subtitle: Infrastructure and community development initiatives across Kaura Local Government Area.

projectsPage.introParagraph: Kaura Local Government continues to invest in roads, schools, healthcare, and agricultural support to improve the quality of life for its citizens.

hero.title: Agriculture in Kaura
hero.subtitle: Agriculture remains the backbone of Kaura's economy.

agriculturePage.overview: Farmers across Kaura cultivate maize, ginger, millet, sorghum, and groundnuts while raising livestock to support household income and food supply.
